name: PCI DSS (payment card data)
description: Guards for codebases near cardholder data: PAN literals, CVV storage, card data in logs, magstripe track data. Supports PCI DSS requirement 3/4 evidence — evidence, not certification.

policies.name: no-card-number-literals
policies.rule: Payment card numbers (PANs) must never appear as literals in source code or fixtures.
policies.skill: Use the official test PANs from your payment provider's sandbox docs (e.g. Stripe's 4242... tokens) — never a number that could be real.
policies.fixSuggestion: Replace with your payment provider's documented sandbox test card numbers.

policies.name: no-cvv-storage
policies.rule: CVV/CVC must never be stored — assignment or persistence of card verification values is prohibited (PCI DSS 3.2).
policies.skill: CVV may only transit directly to the payment processor. Never assign it to a model field, database column, cache, or log.
policies.fixSuggestion: Pass CVV straight to the processor SDK call; never store or assign it.

policies.name: no-card-data-in-logs
policies.rule: Card numbers and cardholder data must not be written to logs.
policies.skill: Log the processor's token or the last4 field your provider returns — never the PAN, expiry, or cardholder name together.
policies.fixSuggestion: Log the payment token or last4 instead of raw card data.

policies.name: no-track-data
policies.rule: Magnetic stripe track data must never be handled or stored in application code.
policies.skill: Track data belongs exclusively to the payment terminal and processor. Application code referencing track1/track2 is a design problem — raise it.
policies.fixSuggestion: Remove track-data handling; only the payment terminal/processor may touch it.
